page: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 for vector neural network instructions, AES cryptography acceleration, and Intel Hyper-Threading.

The instance is configured with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. This memory density is paired with 1229 GB of local SSD storage, and the instance is Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance external disk attachments. This combination of high memory capacity and fast local storage supports data-intensive operations without relying solely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data places the E32ds_v5 in the average performance tier across most compute and memory workloads. It achieves a Geekbench multi-core score of 13847.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 36757.75. Memory bandwidth tests show 143.87 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 67.31 GB/sec for write operations. In database-specific testing, the instance handles 31.13 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 15337.83 on Passmark database operations. For small LLM inference tasks, it demonstrates strong text generation performance at 278.54 tokens per second, though performance drops to 6.67 tokens per second for prompt processing on larger 70B models due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E32ds_v5 offers a balanced resource density for memory-bound workloads, mitigating the need to provision larger, more expensive compute-heavy instances just to satisfy RAM requirements. The inclusion of a 1229 GB local disk adds value for workloads requiring temporary scratch space or high-speed caching. This server is best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring a high memory-to-CPU ratio.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It provides 32 dedicated vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance supports AVX-512 and AES instructions. Benchmark results show solid mid-tier performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 36757.75 and a multi-core Geekbench score of 13847.0. It excels in memory-intensive tasks, delivering 31.13 million Redis SET operations per second and 143.87 GB/sec large-block read memory bandwidth. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes it highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads, avoiding the need to over-provision CPU resources. This instance is recommended for relational databases, in-memory caching, data analytics, and enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, designed for database workloads and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high-density RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processors, and fast local SSD storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM providing an 8:1 memory-to-core ratio.
Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor with AVX-512 instruction support.
Includes 1229 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage.
Delivers 31.13 million Redis SET operations per second in benchmark testing.
Optimized for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data analytics.
